agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Higher nonprofit branch density indicates supportive local philanthropic ecosystems or agglomeration effects, boosting log-linear fundraising efficiency.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Okten & Weisbrod (2000) discuss returns to scale and clustering effects.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant positive correlation was found. An increase in nonprofit branch density is associated with a 1.25% increase in log fundraising efficiency, showing positive agglomeration spillover.
